items.title: Multi-class neural classification
items.description: Softmax outputs define Q; training minimizes average −log q(y) over labeled data — the workhorse objective from logistic regression through transformers.

items.title: Language modeling and LLM pretraining
items.description: Next-token prediction minimizes cross-entropy over the vocabulary at each position; token-level perplexity derives directly from average cross-entropy.

items.title: Binary and multi-label tasks
items.description: Sigmoid + binary cross-entropy handles spam detection, toxicity flags, and independent multi-label heads where each label is a Bernoulli trial.

items.title: Knowledge distillation
items.description: Student models can match teacher soft distributions via cross-entropy between teacher probabilities and student softmax — a KL-related training signal on fixed teacher P.

items.title: Evaluation metrics
items.description: Log loss / cross-entropy on held-out data measures probabilistic quality beyond accuracy; lower values indicate better calibrated probability on true classes.
